name: Poppyseed Pigtails
recipeInstructions:
DESCRIPTION: A rich, sour-cream dough is laden with poppyseeds and braided into 5-inch sticks.
1. Combine flour, poppy seed, and baking soda; set aside.
2. Beat together butter, sugar, and salt until combined.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition.
4. Beat in sour cream and vanilla. Add flour in 1-cup increments, blending after each addition. Gather dough into a ball and wrap in plastic; refrigerate for one hour.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Divide dough in half; refrigerate one half.
6. On a well-floured surface, roll out half of the dough into a rectangle measuring 15 inches by 5 inches, 1/4 inch thick.
7. Using a floured knife, cut the dough into 60 strips, each measuring 5 inches long and 1/4 inch wide. Lay three 5-inch strips side by side on the floured surface.
8. Braid the strips (working from the middle toward the ends to create a more uniform braid), pinching the ends together.
9. Brush the completed braids with egg white glaze and sprinkle lightly with poppyseed.
10. Arrange the braids, spaced 1 inch apart, on buttered baking sheets. Repeat with the second half of dough.
11. Bake for 13-16 minutes, or until golden and crisp. Cool on racks. Store airtight at room temperature for up to 5 days, or freeze for up to three months.
